items.title: Watch: Match of the Day
items.summary: Highlights of the day's five Premier League matches, including Leeds United v Arsenal.

items.title: Steijn rekent op Lauritsen en Kitolano, Sparta wil nog buitenspeler
items.summary: De winterse transfermarkt is geopend. In dit blog vind je alle speculaties en bevestigde transfers in Nederland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in het buitenland.

items.title: Konaté scoort voor zijn overleden vader: 'Van Slot hoefde ik me niet te haasten'
items.summary: De wedstrijd Liverpool - Newcastle United kreeg een prachtig sluitstuk in de vorm van een goal van Ibrahima Konaté, die deze maand zijn vader verloor. De centrumverdediger kwam aan het woord na een kippenvelmoment.

items.title: Geprezen De Jong zelfkritisch: 'Ik had nog meer assists kunnen geven'
items.summary: Frenkie de Jong maakte zaterdagavond indruk bij Barcelona met een prachtige assist in het uitduel met Elche , maar de Oranje-international was na afloop niet helemaal tevreden over zijn inbreng. Spaanse media waren wel lovend over de middenvelder.

items.title: Rashford and Yamal on target in Barcelona win over Elche
Ekitike double helps Liverpool cruise past Newcastle
items.summary: Summer signing Huge Ekitike scores twice as Liverpool record their first league win of 2026, putting four goals past Newcastle United.

items.title: Chelsea fight back to beat West Ham in dramatic derby
items.summary: Chelsea come back from being two goals down at half-time to beat West Ham in stoppage time.

items.title: Kroupi scores stunner as Bournemouth beat Wolves
items.summary: 19-year-old Eli Juniour Kroupi scores his eight Premier League goal of the season to help Bournemouth beat Wolves 2-0.

items.title: Arsenal score four in superb win against Leeds
items.summary: Arsenal get their title charge back on track with a dominant display against Leeds, their first win in four Premier League games.

items.title: Papers: Carrick keen to bring Rashford back to Man Utd
items.summary: The top stories and transfer rumours from Sunday's newspapers...

items.title: Basisspeler Teze boekt belangrijke zege met AS Monaco
items.summary: Welkom in de VI Live van zaterdag 31 januari. Blik hier terug op de hoogtepunten van deze dag uit onder meer de Eredivisie, de Premier League, La Liga en de Bundesliga.

items.title: Korda leads in Florida after superb round in wind as England's Woad falters
items.summary: Nelly Korda stormed into a three-shot lead at the Hilton Grand Vacations Tournament of Champions in her push for a first title in over a year as England's halfway leader Lottie Woad saw her hopes of victory fade after a day of bogeys in blustery Florida.

items.title: Ekitiké and Wirtz sink Newcastle before Konaté seals welcome Liverpool win
items.summary: You could say Hugo Ekitiké was the one who got away from Newcastle last summer, except there were so many. The Liverpool striker gave Eddie Howe further cause for regret with a gamechanging, match-winning performance at Anfield, however, as Arne Slot’s side found the consistency that has eluded them too often this season to record a second impressive win in succession. Ekitiké struck twice against the club that had agreed a deal to sign him from Eintracht Frankfurt before Liverpool swept in. Their initial outlay of £69m, rising to £79m, is proving money very well spent. Newcastle were the dominant force before Ekitiké’s swift brace transformed the contest. Florian Wirtz added a third in the second half before Ibrahima Konaté, playing for the first time since the death of his father, capitalised on a Nick Pope error to complete an important victory. Konaté was understandably in tears as the moment sunk in. Continue reading...

items.title: Veelbesproken Vaessen de schlemiel bij Groningen: Sparta klimt naar plek vijf
items.summary: Sparta Rotterdam is zaterdag naar plek vijf in de Eredivisie geklommen. Tegen directe concurrent FC Groningen boekte de ploeg van trainer Maurice Steijn de vijfde competitiezege op rij.

items.title: Hearts surge seven points clear after brushing aside nine-man Dundee United
items.summary: Pierre Landry Kabore scored a double as William Hill Premiership leaders Hearts cranked up the pressure on title rivals Rangers and Celtic by easing to a 3-0 away win over nine-man Dundee United.

items.title: Ekitike and Wirtz shine as vintage Liverpool return to beat Newcastle
items.summary: Hugo Ekitike scored twice as Liverpool produced one of their most convincing performances of the season to come from behind to beat Newcastle 4-1, rising above Manchester United into fifth in the Premier League table.

items.title: 0-2 at HT, 3-2 at FT! Chelsea in epic comeback win...
items.summary: Chelsea came from two goals down to seal a remarkable 3-2 victory over relegation-threatened West Ham United on Saturday.

items.title: Arsenal thrash Leeds to reinstate 7-point PL lead
items.summary: Arsenal have reinstated their seven-point lead at the top of the Premier League after a 4-0 win against Leeds United.

items.title: Snoop Dogg wants to make Swansea 'global name'
items.summary: Swansea City investor Snoop Dogg has said he wants to take the side back to the Premier League, and establish them as a 'global name'.

items.title: Messi, CR7 jerseys headline Terry's $695K auction
items.summary: Former England and Chelsea captain John Terry netted $695,000 from a 50-item auction of his personal collection, headlined by match-worn Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo jerseys.
